engine.description: Deterministic Git snapshot, compact evidence routing, packs, checkpoints, doctor, installers, and hooks.
contracts: The canonical engine remains Python stdlib-only.
Normal start is read-only with respect to tracked repo files until setup is explicit.
Git filenames are parsed losslessly enough for Unicode, spaces, renames, and Unix absolute hook paths.
Generated packs are routing hints and never replace source verification.
Safe hooks warn and fail open instead of blocking commits.
Agent evidence is bounded, line-numbered current source and avoids duplicate source copies.
failure_modes: First-run orientation creates AGENTS.md, .gitignore, or .context-pack without consent.
Context Pack metadata hides the product files being reviewed.
Normal start scans every text file just to print an estimate.
A hook records a broken relative engine path or propagates a safe-mode failure.
Custom area names fall back to overview because roles are inferred only from literal IDs.
Search-only routing adds a model turn without removing the next grep or file-read turn.

distribution.description: Python/npm packaging, synchronized resources, CI, release workflow, and install smoke tests.
contracts: Canonical engine, skill, agent metadata, and plugin manifest are synchronized before test, build, and release.
The npm wrapper delegates to the bundled Python engine and does not duplicate routing logic.
Packaged validation exercises transient start, configured work, review, checkpoint, and install-codex.
Registry publishing remains opt-in until trusted publishing is configured.
failure_modes: A package ships stale embedded instructions or engine code.
A source-checkout test passes while the wheel or npm tarball fails.
Version metadata drifts across Python, npm, engine, and plugin files.
Release assets are built from a different revision than the tag.

docs-adoption.description: Public positioning, onboarding, benchmark evidence, demo, changelog, and release notes.
contracts: English and Korean onboarding describe the same product behavior.
Benchmark claims distinguish approximate orientation budgets from billed tokens and patch quality.
Actual Codex usage reports total and uncached input, quality, command count, tool output, and latency separately and does not claim universal token savings.
Negative transient results remain visible beside maintained-context results.
The main README keeps one primary install path per agent surface and links details instead of repeating them.
failure_modes: README output or screenshots describe an older CLI flow.
A ratio is promoted as actual token billing or task-quality evidence.
Internal audit notes crowd out the five-minute getting-started path.

skill-plugin.description: Codex skill instructions, agent metadata, and plugin-facing natural-language behavior.
contracts: The agent interprets natural-language task, review, continuation, and handoff intent.
The CLI receives explicit operations and does not maintain a duplicate phrase classifier.
Configured repositories may use Context Pack automatically; unconfigured repositories use normal code search unless a transient preview is explicitly requested.
Skill instructions stay short enough to be cheap background guidance.
Agents use compact current-source Evidence directly instead of reopening shown ranges.
failure_modes: The skill turns every small task into Context Pack ceremony.
The plugin promises automatic lifecycle behavior that the skill cannot enforce.
Packaged skill content drifts from the canonical SKILL.md.
An inferred transient route finds a related file but distracts the agent from the actual defect.

tests.description: Unit and integration coverage for engine behavior and release-critical flows.
contracts: Tests exercise real Git repositories and command entrypoints.
Hook coverage executes the installed hook, not only its generated text.
Resource sync and packaged CLI behavior are release gates.
failure_modes: Windows-only tests miss broken Unix absolute paths.
ASCII-only fixtures miss Korean branches or filenames.
Source imports pass while installed package entrypoints fail.

overview.description: Project orientation, shared handoff state, and repository-level agent rules.
contracts: Context docs are routing hints, not ground truth.
Shared handoff state names the exact repository and version being resumed.
failure_modes: A session edits the wrong checkout or trusts a stale summary over source.
Overview context dominates a task that has a specific source or test route.
